Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment. Do not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed. A full-time position normally corresponds to 37.5 hours a week. total hours per employee will be 37.5 hours multiplied by 52 weeks which equals 1950 hours per year per employee. If an employee dies or is seriously injured in an occupational accident, the employer must immediately and as quickly as possible notify the Norwegian Labor Inspection Authority and the nearest police authority. The employer must confirm the notice in writing. The protection representative must have a copy of the confirmation.\nhttps://arbeidsmiljoloven.com/paragraf/arbeidsgivers-varslings-og-meldeplikt/

Examples of major injuries include broken bones, amputations, unconsciousness and death. They also include injuries that would result in not being able to attend work for more than 3 consecutive days.

Minor injuries are typically those injuries not classified as a major such as a paper cut, twisted ankle, bruising or knock.

Avetta Norway Annual Update

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.
